content.problemStatement: Given the head of a singly linked list containing integers, shift the elements of the linked list to the right by k places and return the head of the modified list. Do not change the values of the nodes, only change the links between nodes.
content.inputFormat: The input will contain the head of a singly linked list and an integer k. The head will be represented as a list of integers, and k will be a single integer.
content.outputFormat: The output will be the head of the modified linked list after rotating it to the right by k places.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a two-pointer approach to rotate the linked list to the right by k places. We will first find the length of the linked list and then connect the last node to the head to form a circular linked list. Then, we will find the new tail node and break the circular linked list at that point to get the rotated linked list.
